name: feynman-loop
description: Explain it back: get judged against your own source and keep a verified knowledge graph of what you actually know.
long_description: The understanding layer for AI workflows. You explain a concept in your own words; Feynman-Loop scores it against a fixed rubric grounded in your own source, returns your gaps as questions (never answers), tests whether you can APPLY the concept, and resurfaces it when it is due. Everything is stored in a local ledger and rendered as an Obsidian-compatible knowledge graph. Works with or without an API key: without one, your own chat model judges under a verified-evidence protocol and every score is computed in code.

anthropic_api_key.title: Anthropic API key (optional)
anthropic_api_key.description: Leave empty for zero-key mode: your own chat model judges under a verified protocol, with all scores computed in code. With a key, an independent judge model scores instead (strongest).

tools.name: quick_check
tools.description: The 2-minute volley: one sharp question per rubric point, one-line answers, instant verdicts

tools.name: answer
tools.description: Submit the learner's answer to the current volley question; returns the verdict and next question

tools.name: start_check
tools.description: Start a full explain-back check on a concept, grounded in source text from the conversation

tools.name: judge_explanation
tools.description: Score the learner's explanation against the concept's fixed rubric; gaps come back as questions

tools.name: make_transfer
tools.description: Generate a novel application challenge once the explanation is solid

tools.name: score_transfer
tools.description: Score the transfer answer; one bounded retry if it falls short

tools.name: progress
tools.description: The persistent understanding ledger: every concept, what is due, the learner profile

tools.name: journey
tools.description: Every past attempt on a concept in the learner's own words

tools.name: knowledge_map
tools.description: The verified knowledge graph as a mermaid diagram plus an Obsidian vault

tools.name: submit_rubric
tools.description: Zero-key protocol: store the host-built rubric after server-side verification

tools.name: submit_judgment
tools.description: Zero-key protocol: submit verdicts; evidence verified and score computed in code

tools.name: submit_transfer_probe
tools.description: Zero-key protocol: store the host-built transfer challenge after verification
